LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC'S PETITION TO (1) COMPEL ARBITRATION AND (2) STAY ACTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1281.2 AND 9 U.S.C. SECTION 4. IS DENIED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IN THE CLIENT AGREEMENT IS APPLICABLE TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY (MSSB), THAT CLAUSE DOES NOT COVER CLAIMS BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND M. LEGG. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST MSSB AND MS. LEGG ARE INTERRELATED (SEE THIRD THROUGH SEVENTH CLAIMS IN THE COMPLAINT) AND THERE IS A REAL POSSIBILITY OF CONFLICTING RULINGS IF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST MS. LEGG AND MORGAN STANLEY ARE DECIDED IN TWO DIFFERENT FORA. (CCP 1281.2(C)). IN PARTICULAR, IF THE MOTION WAS GRANTED, THEN PLAINTIFF WOULD FACE THE RISK THAT IN DIFFERENT FORA MS. LEGG AND MSSB WOULD DEFEND BASED ON THE ALLEGED MISCONDUCT OF THE OTHER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
ORDER RE: DEFENDANT MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JANUARY 17, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL SECOND THROUGH SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION WITH TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. AN ACCOUNTING CLAIM IS NOT APPLICABLE UNDER THE FACTS OF THIS CASE. ALL THE OTHER CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Sep-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: matthew k. hindman, esq., mastromonaco real property law group, 415.956.4030 x2, for defendants lorraine o. legg and 3234 washington street associates; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS LORRAINE O. LEGG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GENERAL PARTNER OF 3234 WASHINGTON STREET ASSOCIATES, and 3234 WASHINGTON STREET ASSOCIATES' Motion For Leave To Amend Answer To First Amended Complaint is Granted on the following conditions: 1) plaintiff has until 7 days before trial to conduct discovery on the new affirmative defenses and cross-complaint and may propound written discovery as to those matters requiring responses within ten days; 2) Plaintiff may take a second deposition of Ms. Legg on the new affirmative defenses and cross-complaint; and 3) plaintiff may file a motion for summary judgment as to the new affirmative defenses and cross-complaint per the schedule set forth in Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b) and may notice the motion to be heard on or before October 29, 2012. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-29-2011 Continued To Jan-13-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Defendant Morgan Stanley Smith Barney, Llc'S Petition To 1) Compel Arbitration And 2) Stay Action
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC'S PETITION TO (1) COMPEL ARBITRATION AND (2) STAY ACTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1281.2 AND 9 U.S.C. SECTION 4.

APPEARANCES: Jordan A. Sussman, Esq. of Zief, Diment & Glickman 986-3644 for pltf & opposing party; Braden O. Wilhelm, Esq. of Morgan Stanley Smith Barney 693-7480 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Denied. Assuming without deciding that the arbitration clause in the Client Agreement is applicable to plaintiff's claims against Morgan Stanley Smith Barney (MSSB), that clause does not cover claims between plaintiff and M. Legg. Plaintiff's claims against MSSB and Ms. Legg are interrelated (see third through seventh claims in the complaint) and there is a real possibility of conflicting rulings if plaintiff's claims against Ms. Legg and Morgan Stanley are decided in two different fora. (CCP 1281.2(c)). In particular, if the motion was granted, then plaintiff would face the risk that in different fora Ms. Legg and MSSB would defend based on the alleged misconduct of the other. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Ntc Of Petition And Petition To 1) Compel Arbitration And 2) Stay Action
